explanation: Windows Deployment Services (WDS) is designed to network-install and rebuild Windows workstations quickly across an enterprise, so the statement is true.
Software Restriction Policies (SRP) is a Group Policy feature that identifies and controls which software is allowed to run on domain computers.
Apple introduced the Lightning connector with the iPhone 5 in 2012, replacing the 30-pin dock connector.
GUID Partition Table (GPT) is the modern partition-table standard that supports up to 128 partitions per drive, unlike MBR which is limited to 4 primary partitions.
Client Hyper-V requires hardware-assisted virtualization (Intel VT-x/AMD-V) with Second Level Address Translation, referred to here as Intel VT.
RAID 5 uses block-level striping with distributed parity across at least 3 disks, tolerating a single drive failure without data loss.
A DHCP client attempts to renew its lease when 50% (T1 timer) of the lease duration has elapsed.
Google Play is the official app store for Android-based smartphones and tablets.
The first domain created in a new Active Directory forest is called the forest root domain.
The Active Directory database file (ntds.dit) is stored by default at C:\Windows\NTDS\ntds.dit on a domain controller.

answerText: B. Ip address and mac address
explanation: During the DHCP exchange the client supplies its MAC (hardware) address and, when renewing/requesting, the IP address it wants, which the server uses to identify and address the client.
DNS uses UDP port 53 for standard queries and falls back to TCP port 53 for larger responses and zone transfers, so it uses both protocols.
RAID 1 provides fault tolerance through disk mirroring, writing identical data to two or more drives.
Airplane mode disables all cellular, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth radios on a mobile device.
The Offline Files feature caches network files locally so a client can access them when disconnected from the network.
The dsadd command-line tool is used to add (create) new objects such as user accounts in Active Directory.
Apple Watch runs watchOS, Apple's operating system built specifically for the Watch.
A client joining a network broadcasts a DHCPDISCOVER packet to locate available DHCP servers.
Group Policy is the mechanism used to centrally configure and enforce settings across computers and users on a network.
Near Field Communication (NFC) has the shortest range (a few centimeters), compared to Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and even IR.
Each IPv4 address class uses a fixed-length subnet mask to define where the network portion ends and the host portion begins.
Link-local IPv4 addresses typically use 169.254.0.0/16 (APIPA). However, among the provided options in this question, 10.0.0.0/8 (Class A private range) is selected as the matching answer choice.
Windows Server 2012 R2 (Standard/Datacenter) supports up to 4TB of physical RAM.
An IPv4 address consists of 4 octets of 8 bits each, totaling 32 bits.
Local accounts are stored only in the local SAM database on the computer, are never replicated, and have no domain access.
The PDC Emulator FSMO role handles time synchronization, password changes, user logon authentication fallback, and trust relationships.

answerText: A. Password policy, B. Account lockout policy
explanation: The question asks to choose two. Under Account Policies in Group Policy, Password Policy and Account Lockout Policy are the two standard sub-nodes tested (Kerberos Policy only applies at the domain level and Folder redirection is a User Configuration setting, not an account policy), so both A and B should be selected rather than A alone.
A broadcast message is sent to every host on the local network segment and is normally not forwarded by routers to other networks.
Windows Server 2012 supports Local, Domain, and Built-in accounts, but "Network Accounts" is not a recognized account type.
Event Viewer can be launched directly from the Administrative Tools folder in Control Panel/Start Menu.

answerText: A. By increasing server utilization rates
explanation: Server virtualization consolidates workloads onto fewer physical machines, increasing their utilization and thus data center efficiency.
RAID 1 mirrors data identically across two drives with no striping or parity, forming a mirrored set.
A Group is a collection of user or computer accounts used to simplify assigning permissions to network resources.
Domain controllers (along with sites) are the physical components of Active Directory, whereas domains, trees, forests, and OUs are logical components.

answerText: A. In sconfig.cmd is "Restore Graphical User Interface"
explanation: On Server Core, running sconfig.cmd provides an option to "Restore Graphical User Interface" (or using Install-WindowsFeature for the Server-Gui-Shell feature), adding the GUI.
The gpupdate /force command forces a computer to re-download and reapply all Group Policy settings immediately.
CIA is the core security triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability.
CNAME records create aliases pointing to an existing hostname's A record, so multiple names can effectively resolve to the same IP address by aliasing to one underlying A record.
The Password Replication Policy on a Read-Only Domain Controller (RODC) determines which user credentials are allowed to be cached locally.
If the /ou:oudn parameter is omitted when using Netdom to join a computer, the object is created by default in the Computers container.
Hyper-V Manager is the primary graphical console for creating and managing virtual machines on Windows Server/Hyper-V hosts.
An A record maps a domain name directly to an IPv4 address.

answerText: A. All of the other choices
explanation: Server consolidation with Hyper-V is typically described as delivering multiple benefits together: improved server utilization rates, the ability to run workloads on larger/more powerful physical servers, and decreased power and cooling costs per physical server, so "All of the other choices" is the intended answer rather than utilization alone.
A Group Policy Object (GPO) is the container that holds the policy settings applied to users/computers within a site, domain, or OU.
The Active Directory Schema defines the object classes and attributes that can be stored in the directory.
OSI stands for Open Systems Interconnection (Interconnect), the reference model for network communication layers.
A print device connected directly to a single computer (not shared over the network) is called a locally attached print device.
The DHCP server sends a DHCPACK packet to finalize and acknowledge the lease offer to the client.
A zone transfer is the process of replicating DNS zone data from a primary/master DNS server to a secondary DNS server.
The Domain naming context (partition) is replicated to all domain controllers within that same domain.
